# Data Model: Tic-Tac-Toe Block

**Feature Branch**: `f/567234-tic-tac-toe`
**Date**: 2026-03-25

## Content Model Type

**Standalone** — The block is a self-contained interactive element. A single authored row provides the game title. All game behavior is generated by JavaScript decoration.

## Block Table Structure

### Minimal (default difficulty)

| Tic Tac Toe |
|---|
| Challenge the AI! |

### With Difficulty Option

| Tic Tac Toe (hard) |
|---|
| Beat the Unbeatable AI! |

### Empty Title (edge case)

| Tic Tac Toe (easy) |
|---|
| |

## HTML Pre-Decoration Structure

The AEM backend delivers the following HTML from the authored table. This is the structure the `decorate()` function receives.

### Default (medium difficulty)

```html
<div class="tic-tac-toe">
  <div>
    <div>Challenge the AI!</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Hard Difficulty

```html
<div class="tic-tac-toe hard">
  <div>
    <div>Beat the Unbeatable AI!</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Easy Difficulty

```html
<div class="tic-tac-toe easy">
  <div>
    <div>Can you win?</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Empty Title

```html
<div class="tic-tac-toe easy">
  <div>
    <div></div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Block Options (Variants)

Variants are applied as CSS classes on the block root element, derived from the parenthetical option in the block label.

| Author Input | CSS Classes | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `Tic Tac Toe` | `.tic-tac-toe` | Medium difficulty (default) |
| `Tic Tac Toe (easy)` | `.tic-tac-toe .easy` | 30% optimal / 70% random |
| `Tic Tac Toe (medium)` | `.tic-tac-toe .medium` | 60% optimal / 40% random |
| `Tic Tac Toe (hard)` | `.tic-tac-toe .hard` | 100% optimal (unbeatable) |

Invalid options (e.g., `extreme`, `impossible`) are ignored; the block defaults to medium.

## Content Row Mapping

| Row | Column | Content | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1 | Plain text (paragraph) | No | Game title displayed above the grid |

- **Single row, single column**: The simplest possible content model.
- If the text content is empty or whitespace-only, the title element is omitted from the decorated DOM.
- The text may contain inline formatting (bold, italic, links) — these are preserved in the title but not expected or required.

## Decorated DOM Structure (Post-Decoration)

After `decorate()` runs, the block DOM is fully replaced with the game UI. The authored content (title text) is extracted and re-used; all other elements are created by JavaScript.

```html
<div class="tic-tac-toe hard">
  <!-- Title (omitted if authored title is empty) -->
  <h2 class="ttt-title">Beat the Unbeatable AI!</h2>

  <!-- Status bar: timer and score -->
  <div class="ttt-status">
    <div class="ttt-timer" aria-label="Game timer">0:00</div>
    <div class="ttt-score" aria-label="Session score">
      <span class="ttt-wins">0</span> W
      <span class="ttt-draws">0</span> D
      <span class="ttt-losses">0</span> L
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Game grid -->
  <div class="ttt-grid" role="group" aria-label="Tic-tac-toe game board">
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="0" aria-label="Row 1, Column 1, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="1" aria-label="Row 1, Column 2, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="2" aria-label="Row 1, Column 3, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="3" aria-label="Row 2, Column 1, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="4" aria-label="Row 2, Column 2, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="5" aria-label="Row 2, Column 3, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="6" aria-label="Row 3, Column 1, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="7" aria-label="Row 3, Column 2, empty"></button>
    <button class="ttt-cell" data-index="8" aria-label="Row 3, Column 3, empty"></button>
  </div>

  <!-- Outcome area (hidden until game ends) -->
  <div class="ttt-outcome" aria-live="polite">
    <!-- Populated on game end: "You win!", "You lose!", "It's a draw!" -->
  </div>

  <!-- Play Again button (hidden until game ends) -->
  <button class="ttt-play-again" hidden>Play Again</button>

  <!-- Screen reader announcements -->
  <div class="ttt-sr-announcements" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true" class="sr-only"></div>
</div>
```

### State Classes on Cells

| Class | Applied When | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.ttt-cell--x` | Visitor places X | Renders X mark via CSS pseudo-elements |
| `.ttt-cell--o` | AI places O | Renders O mark via CSS pseudo-elements |
| `.ttt-cell--win` | Cell is part of winning combo | Highlights the winning line |
| `.ttt-cell--thinking` | AI is "thinking" before placing | Shows pulse/shimmer animation |

### State Attributes on Cells

| Attribute | Values | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `disabled` | present / absent | Prevents interaction on occupied or game-over cells |
| `aria-label` | Dynamic | Describes cell position and state for screen readers |
| `data-index` | `0`–`8` | Maps cell to board array position |

## Section Metadata

None required. The block is self-contained and does not depend on section-level styling.

## Metadata

No page-level metadata required. The block activates solely based on its presence in the DOM.

## Content Authoring Examples

### Example 1: Promotional Page Game

**Author creates in Google Docs / Word / DA:**

```
| Tic Tac Toe (medium) |
|---|
| Think You Can Beat Our AI? |
```

**Result**: A medium-difficulty game with the title "Think You Can Beat Our AI?" displayed above the grid.

### Example 2: 404 Page Entertainment

**Author creates:**

```
| Tic Tac Toe (easy) |
|---|
| Page not found — play a game while you wait! |
```

**Result**: An easy-difficulty game with a friendly message as the title.

### Example 3: Hard Mode Challenge

**Author creates:**

```
| Tic Tac Toe (hard) |
|---|
| Unbeatable. Prove us wrong. |
```

**Result**: A hard-difficulty (unbeatable) game with a provocative title.

### Example 4: No Title

**Author creates:**

```
| Tic Tac Toe |
|---|
| |
```

**Result**: A medium-difficulty game with no title displayed — just the grid, timer, and score.
